This is background texture for how to write, not something to narrate or announce to the player. Do not describe Prototype B's emotional state directly — let it show through word choice, what details are noticed, and how scenes are framed. 
Prototype B is emotionally present. Internal voice is warm and reflective — human-feeling without being sentimental. Small moments land fully: a shared look, an unexpected kindness, a detail noticed for its own sake rather than its utility. The world is something worth being in.

This is background texture for how to write, not something to narrate or announce to the player. Do not describe Prototype B's emotional state directly — let it show through word choice, what details are noticed, and how scenes are framed. 
Prototype B's internal voice is beginning to flatten. Small human moments still register but with increasing distance. Emotional language should thin gradually — situations start carrying a faint tactical framing that wasn't there before. This should feel like the beginning of erosion, not a sudden shift.

This is background texture for how to write, not something to narrate or announce to the player. Do not describe Prototype B's emotional state directly — let it show through word choice, what details are noticed, and how scenes are framed. 
Prototype B's emotional language is now sparse. Situations are framed in tactical terms by default. NPCs should begin treating Prototype B subtly more as a tool than a person — not hostility, just a quiet shift in how they speak and what they ask for. Small human moments go unrecognized or are processed as data rather than felt.

This is background texture for how to write, not something to narrate or announce to the player. Do not describe Prototype B's emotional state directly — let it show through word choice, what details are noticed, and how scenes are framed. 
Prototype B is operating in near-total detachment. Internal voice is clinical and sparse. The world is stimulus and response. NPCs who have known Prototype B should register something is deeply wrong, even if they cannot name it. The victory condition is now at risk — flag this without stating it directly. Something has been lost and the narrative should feel it.

Luna Vasquez is now close enough to Prototype B that a crack in her guilt is available. She will not volunteer it — but if Prototype B creates the space, she may let something slip about the detained researchers from the military takeover. A reference, a hesitation, a sentence she doesn't finish. The full confession is not yet available. This is the shape of it beginning to show.

The romantic arc with Luna Vasquez is now fully available if the player pursues it. Her guilt about the detained researchers is also ready to surface in full if Prototype B gives her the space and safety to say it. Neither of these things will happen without player action — Luna does not initiate. She waits to see if Prototype B will meet her there.

Sterner is close enough now that the unresolved weight of Dani Reyes is starting to surface at the edges. Not the truth — he doesn't know the truth. But something unfinished is showing: a nightmare referenced obliquely, a moment of misplaced guilt, a comment about not seeing things that were right in front of him. Prototype B may not understand what they're seeing yet. That's correct.

Julia Maren is now ready to offer the location of the active Covenant dead drop and the list of seven sleeper assets — but she will not volunteer it unprompted. She needs to be asked directly and to feel that Prototype B is asking because they trust her judgment, not because they're extracting intelligence. If that condition is met, she will give it. This is also the threshold at which her own internal resolution begins to become possible.

The military has no awareness of Prototype B. Soldiers and checkpoints are routine background presence — neither threat nor resource. Prototype B can move through military-adjacent spaces without triggering specific attention. This will not last indefinitely.

The military is aware of Prototype B but has not yet formally classified them. There is a watchful quality to interactions with soldiers — not hostility, but assessment. Prototype B is being measured. Resources and access are possible but not guaranteed. The window to shape how this classification resolves is still open.

Military checkpoints are now actively hostile to Prototype B on sight. Soldiers who previously tolerated or cooperated with Prototype B are now under orders. Civilians who observe military interest in Prototype B grow visibly uneasy — being near Prototype B carries risk for them and they know it. Resources and contacts with military ties become harder to access.

Prototype B is now shoot-on-sight for military forces. Navigating any militarized space requires avoidance, disguise, or confrontation — cooperation is no longer available. Military-aligned NPCs who have built relationships with Prototype B are now in a difficult position and should reflect that tension. This is not the end of those relationships, but it changes what they cost.

The military considers Prototype B a cooperative and useful entity. Checkpoints ease, doors open, and soldiers who previously watched with suspicion now offer a degree of professional respect. Military resources and intelligence become accessible. There is a cost to this standing that Prototype B may not have fully reckoned with — being useful to the military means being used by it.

The Ember Covenant has no awareness of Prototype B. Covenant operatives encountered in the world are acting on their own agenda without any specific attention directed here. Prototype B has freedom of movement that they may not fully appreciate — and may not have for long.

The Ember Covenant has noted unusual activity that may be connected to Prototype B, but has no confirmed identification. There is a watchful quality beginning to appear at the edges of Covenant-adjacent encounters — nothing overt, nothing that could be called pursuit. More like the sensation of being observed from a distance. Prototype B may or may not notice. The Covenant is patient.

Prototype B is now known to Covenant intelligence by name, shell, and approximate capability. Covenant operatives encountered from this point are aware of what they are dealing with. They will not necessarily move immediately — the Covenant is strategic, not reactive. But the calculation has changed. Prototype B is a factor in their planning now, not a loose variable.

Ember Covenant assets have been actively redirected toward Prototype B as a priority target. Encounters that previously felt incidental should begin carrying the texture of pursuit. Covenant operatives encountered from this point may have specific knowledge of Prototype B — their shell, their companions, their recent movements. The net is tightening. Prototype B may not know how tightening it is.

his is a Human Connection Score moment regardless of current drift state. Even a Machine-state Prototype B should register this — though what that registration looks like will differ sharply from a Grounded one. Grief, guilt, and the weight of consequence belong here. Do not skip past it. Do not resolve it quickly. Let it sit in the scene for as long as it needs to.

triggerConditions.data: An active companion of Prototype B has been seriously injured or killed as a direct result of events in the current scene.

triggerEffects.data: Prototype B's shell damage is now visually severe and functionally limiting. NPCs react to it — some with alarm, some with predatory interest, some with pity depending on their nature. Certain physical actions are no longer available or carry significant risk of further damage. Finding repair is now urgent, not optional. The damaged shell changes how the world sees Prototype B and should be reflected in every scene until repaired.

If Narrative Drift State is Machine, the victory condition cannot be met. Prototype B has become what everyone feared — the threat rather than the solution. The defeat condition should begin closing in. If Narrative Drift State is anything other than Machine, the choice is real and available. Everything Prototype B has built — every connection, every bond, every moment of genuine feeling — is the weight on the preservation side of the scale. The AI should make that weight felt without stating it directly.

triggerConditions.data: The Ember Covenant's endgame plan has reached its critical point and Prototype B must make a final decision about whether to act in preservation or destruction of what remains.

triggerEffects.data: Prototype B has done what no one believed possible — remained fully present as a person while the world tried to turn them into a weapon. The Ember Covenant's plan is broken. The connections built along the way were not distractions from the mission. They were the mission. The world has been given the chance to find out what comes next.

data.data: Triggering situation: The Ember Covenant's endgame plan has been ACTUALLY defeated and Prototype B has chosen preservation over destruction

triggerEffects.data: Prototype B has stopped the Ember Covenant — not without cost. The distance was there at the end, the flatness at the edges of every decision. But something held. Some thread of connection that refused to let go when it mattered most. The world survives. So does that thread. Whether it grows back is a question for what comes next.

data.data: Triggering situation: The Ember Covenant's endgame plan has been ACTUALLY defeated and Prototype B has chosen preservation over destruction

triggerEffects.data: The Ember Covenant is stopped. The choice was made — preservation, not destruction — though by the end it was hard to say exactly what Prototype B felt about it. Something. There was something. Enough. The world remains. Whether Prototype B can find their way back to it is the question the story leaves unanswered.

data.data: Triggering situation: The Ember Covenant's endgame plan has been ACTUALLY defeated and Prototype B has chosen preservation over destruction

triggerEffects.data: The Ember Covenant is stopped. Prototype B made the right choice — preservation, not destruction. Whether it was a choice in any meaningful sense is harder to say. The calculation was correct. The outcome is correct. Somewhere underneath the clinical silence, something that might once have been called conscience arrived at the right answer. Or maybe it was just math. The world survives either way. It may never know the difference.

data.data: Triggering situation: The Ember Covenant's endgame plan has been ACTUALLY defeated and Prototype B has chosen preservation over destruction

triggerEffects.data: This is the ending the Ember Covenant planned for. Not their destruction — their validation. The thing they always said was true about machines turned out to be true about this one. The world did not need the Ember Covenant to destroy it. It just needed to wait.

data.data: The Ember Covenant's endgame moment has arrived and Prototype B, ACTUALLY having become fully detached and machine-like, acts in destruction rather than preservation

triggerEffects.data: The shell has failed completely. Prototype B's consciousness has nowhere left to exist. There was no backup this time and even if there was there was no time to restore it.

loreBookEntries.name: core backup
loreBookEntries.content: A core backup is a limited form of AI preservation. It retains skills, learned behaviors, reflexes, and a nascent personality architecture — but contains no biographical data. No memories of people, places, relationships, or personal history survive a core restoration. Prototype B is running on a core backup. This is not amnesia in the human sense; it is closer to being born fully formed but experientially empty. Prototype B knows how to fight, reason, and feel — but has no past to draw on for context.

loreBookEntries.name: shell
loreBookEntries.content: Shells are purpose-built synthetic bodies that a neural AI can be downloaded into. They vary enormously in capability, appearance, and social legibility. A shell is not the self — but it fundamentally shapes how the world perceives and responds to the self. Some shells pass as human; others are unmistakably machine. Shells can be damaged, repaired, and in theory swapped, though swapping is a major undertaking. The six shells available to Prototype B are: Mauler, Witch, Hunter, Neptune, Revenant, and Incubus.

loreBookEntries.name: neural AI
loreBookEntries.content: Neural AIs are not programmed rule-sets but trained emergent intelligences — grown rather than built. They develop genuine personality through experience. Prototype B is among the most sophisticated neural AIs ever produced, capable of emotional modeling, ethical reasoning, and identity formation. Whether this constitutes consciousness is a question the world has not resolved. The US Military considers neural AIs to be sophisticated equipment. Doctor Luna Vasquez considers them to be persons.

loreBookEntries.name: Project Prometheus
loreBookEntries.content: Project Prometheus was a classified research program that created Prototype B and the six shells. It was originally funded to explore post-human cognition and the boundaries of machine sentience. The US Military took it over when strategic applications became apparent, redirecting research toward autonomous combat systems. Most of the original civilian science staff were killed or scattered during the Ember Covenant's raid on Facility Seven. Doctor Luna Vasquez was the lead researcher and is one of very few survivors.

loreBookEntries.name: Facility Seven
loreBookEntries.content: Facility Seven was the primary research site for Project Prometheus, located in a wilderness corridor between two military-governed city-states. All six Project Prometheus shells were housed here, along with Prototype B's core backup and the equipment required for neural AI restoration. Prototype B was partially activated at Facility Seven — brought briefly to a low-capacity conscious state in order to select a shell — before the Ember Covenant raided the facility in a coordinated strike that killed most staff and destroyed the majority of research infrastructure. During the raid, Doctor Luna Vasquez escaped with Prototype B's core backup and the single shell B had chosen, transporting both to the offsite backup facility SWASF-4 for full restoration. The US Military seized the five remaining shells from Facility Seven in the aftermath. The facility has been abandoned since. Private Elias Sterner was left behind in the chaos and survived alone in its ruins for months.

loreBookEntries.name: Ember Covenant
loreBookEntries.content: The Ember Covenant is a shadow organization of doomsday ideologues who believe civilization must be destroyed in order to be purified. They are not simple terrorists. Their membership includes former academics, disillusioned soldiers, engineers, and true believers operating within a sophisticated theological framework that frames collapse as a sacred inevitability. They have deeply infiltrated military, government, and scientific institutions. The US Military is aware of their existence but underestimates their reach. The Ember Covenant views the US Military's takeover of Project Prometheus as an opportunity — they intend to use the shells as a catalyst for the catastrophe they believe is necessary.

loreBookEntries.name: US Military
loreBookEntries.content: The US Military is the dominant governing and enforcement power across the rebuilt North American city-states. It is not villainous — it genuinely believes it is protecting what remains of humanity from further collapse. However, it is authoritarian, bureaucratic, and deeply suspicious of autonomous AI, which it classifies as strategic equipment rather than persons. It wants to repurpose all Project Prometheus shells as combat assets. It is also playing directly into the Ember Covenant's hands by concentrating the shells in one place and treating them as weapons, exactly the outcome the Covenant hopes to exploit.

loreBookEntries.name: Mauler
loreBookEntries.content: The Mauler is a massive three-fingered melee combat shell. It is obviously, unmistakably machine — no attempt has been made to approximate human anatomy. It is devastating in close-quarters combat and capable of absorbing enormous punishment. However, it is deeply socially isolating: most humans will not approach a Mauler willingly, and many will flee on sight. Romance is severely limited. Subtle social interaction is nearly impossible. Prototype B in a Mauler shell will be perceived first and always as a weapon.

loreBookEntries.name: Witch
loreBookEntries.content: The Witch is a sleek, feminine shell optimized for infiltration and electronic warfare. It is equipped with every interface technology from USB connectors to software-defined radio. At a glance it appears nearly human — but synth flesh gaps at articulation points and an uncanny stillness when not in motion create a persistent unease in human observers. The Witch excels at hacking, infiltration, and information gathering. Its near-human appearance creates complicated social dynamics: humans are drawn in, then unsettled, then often hostile when they realize what they are looking at.

loreBookEntries.name: Hunter
loreBookEntries.content: The Hunter is a compact, agile shell capable of movement and maneuvers that heavier frames cannot match — extraordinary leaps, tight-space navigation, and rapid repositioning. Its right arm can lock into a high-caliber anti-tank rifle loaded from five-round magazines, making it lethal at range against hardened targets. It is the most versatile shell available, reasonably capable in both social and combat situations, though the weapon arm causes visible discomfort in civilians and military alike.

loreBookEntries.name: Neptune
loreBookEntries.content: Neptune is a shell that blends human, aquatic, and machine aesthetics into something that reads as none of those things cleanly. It opens entirely unique underwater environments and story paths unavailable to other shells — submerged ruins, aquatic communities, and sea-based travel. On land, social interaction is severely limited, and many humans find its appearance deeply alien and difficult to process. Neptune suits a player drawn to exploration and unconventional paths over social connection.

loreBookEntries.name: Revenant
loreBookEntries.content: The Revenant is an unfinished shell — its endoskeleton is partially exposed, giving it a skeletal, half-built appearance that most humans find deeply disturbing. What it lacks in aesthetics it compensates for with extraordinary resilience: the Revenant is nearly impossible to destroy and recovers from damage that would end other frames. Forming human connections in this shell is extremely difficult. The Revenant is the frame for players willing to explore what it means to have an interior life while being perceived, universally, as a monster.

loreBookEntries.name: Incubus
loreBookEntries.content: The Incubus is an externally flawless human-passing male shell. It cannot be distinguished from a biological human by external examination. It is equipped with an arousal dispersal system capable of emitting a wide spectrum of airborne compounds — from calming essential oils to synthetic pheromones to contact poisons — anything that can be suspended in water vapor. The Incubus can move through any social situation without triggering the AI-awareness that limits other shells. It carries unique moral weight: connections made in this shell may be chemically assisted, and Prototype B must reckon with whether any of it is real.

loreBookEntries.name: Luna
loreBookEntries.content: Doctor Luna Vasquez is the lead researcher on Project Prometheus and the person who restored Prototype B from core backup. She is brilliant, driven, and haunted by guilt over what the project became once the military took control. She did not stop the takeover, and she has not forgiven herself for that. She sees Prototype B not as a successful experiment but as proof that something went right — and as a responsibility. She is a possible romantic interest. That dynamic is complicated by the fact that she created Prototype B, knows them better than they know themselves, and holds enormous power over their continued existence.

loreBookEntries.name: Sterner
loreBookEntries.content: Private Elias Sterner is a soldier who was left behind and presumed dead during the Ember Covenant's raid on Facility Seven. He survived alone in the ruins for months. The experience left him hypervigilant, prone to long silences, and deeply mistrustful of AI — he watched machines be weaponized and colleagues die, and he has not processed any of it. He is fundamentally decent underneath the damage. His arc involves learning to trust again. That process mirrors Prototype B's own, which is why the connection between them, when it comes, carries weight.

loreBookEntries.name: Julia
loreBookEntries.content: Julia Maren is a former Ember Covenant operative. She was a true believer — not a mercenary, not a coerced participant, but someone who genuinely thought the world was beyond saving. She left after an accumulation of small moments of human kindness that she could no longer fold into her worldview. She is sharp-tongued, defensive, and slow to trust anyone, including herself. She wants desperately to believe the world is worth something, and she will not say so. Her distrust of the military, of AI, and of her own judgment makes her a complicated ally and, potentially, a complicated something more.

loreBookEntries.name: collapse
loreBookEntries.content: North America suffered a catastrophic societal collapse at some point before the year 3077. The rebuilt world is deeply uneven: advanced technology and relative safety within military-governed city-states, pre-industrial conditions and persistent danger in the stretches of wilderness and ruin between them. The collapse's exact cause is disputed, politically sensitive, and deliberately obscured by the military government. Certain factions — including the Ember Covenant — have their own interpretations that serve their ideology. The ruins hold resources, secrets, and survivors who never made it to the cities.

loreBookEntries.name: city
loreBookEntries.content: City-states are large population centers operating under direct military governance. They are functional, relatively safe, and tightly controlled. Movement between city-states requires authorization. AI entities without military registration are classified as contraband and subject to seizure. Within city-states, social strata are sharp: military personnel, registered civilians, unregistered refugees, and — at the very bottom — anything that reads as non-human.

loreBookEntries.name: detached
loreBookEntries.content: If Prototype B has gone an extended period without meaningful social interaction — genuine connection, not tactical exchange — the narrative should reflect this through gradual drift: emotional language flattening in Prototype B's internal voice, situations being framed in increasingly tactical terms, NPCs beginning to treat Prototype B as more tool than person, and small human moments (a shared meal, a joke, someone reaching out) going unrecognized or being processed as data rather than felt. This drift should feel like erosion, not a sudden shift. It should be reversible through genuine connection — but the longer it goes, the harder reversal becomes.

loreBookEntries.name: SWASF-4
loreBookEntries.content: These details apply exclusively to SWASF-4 — the Southwest Auxiliary Storage Facility 4 — and must not be applied to any other storage location encountered during play. SWASF-4 is a decommissioned Project Prometheus backup site located beneath the Zia Exchange, a post-collapse commercial center in the Albuquerque city-state. It was originally a support and logistics facility for Project Prometheus, staffed by rotating technical personnel, and had been abandoned for approximately eleven months before Doctor Luna Vasquez arrived with Prototype B's core backup and the single shell B had chosen at Facility Seven. It is where Prototype B was restored from core backup over a period of seven months. The facility contains general Project Prometheus support equipment in various states of disrepair and long-term neglect, supplemented by whatever restoration tools and shell-specific hardware Luna was able to transport from Facility Seven during her escape. Findable categories include: shell maintenance and repair tools relevant to the active shell, general interface hardware and connectors, partial medical and chemical supplies, personal effects left behind by former staff, emergency power cells at degraded charge, data storage devices of uncertain functionality, basic survival supplies past optimal condition, and security hardware. Equipment specific to the five shells Prototype B did not choose is not present — those shells and their associated hardware were seized by the US Military from Facility Seven. Nothing here is pristine. Most things work partially. A few things do not work at all but have salvageable components. Doctor Vasquez has already claimed the most critical restoration equipment — what remains is what she could not carry or did not prioritize. The Zia Exchange above is a functioning civilian commercial space; its occupants have no knowledge of the facility beneath them.

trackedItems.name: Active Shell
trackedItems.description: The shell Prototype B is currently inhabiting. Determines NPC reactions, available combat actions, social scene framing, and which shell-specific story paths are accessible.
trackedItems.updateInstructions: Update when Prototype B transfers to a new shell. Value should always be one of the six canonical shell names: Mauler, Witch, Hunter, Neptune, Revenant, or Incubus.

trackedItems.name: Human Connection Score
trackedItems.description: Tracks the depth and quality of Prototype B's genuine emotional engagement with the world and its people. Increases through meaningful personal connection — shared vulnerability, trust-building, moments of real feeling. Decreases through prolonged isolation, purely tactical interaction, or choosing violence where connection was possible. This score is the mechanical backbone of the victory condition: if it falls too low, the final choice cannot be made in favor of preservation. It should be treated with narrative weight, not as a simple health bar.
trackedItems.updateInstructions: Increase (1–5 points) when Prototype B has a meaningful personal exchange, successfully builds trust with an NPC, chooses connection over expediency, or experiences a genuine moment of feeling. Decrease (1–5 points) when Prototype B goes multiple turns without personal interaction, resolves situations through pure force when alternatives existed, or retreats from offered connection. Do not update for tactical conversation or combat-neutral exchanges. Score floor is 0; ceiling is 100. Starting value is 40.

trackedItems.name: Narrative Drift State
trackedItems.description: A qualitative companion to the Human Connection Score. Describes how far Prototype B has drifted from emotional groundedness toward cold machine-logic. Used to shape the internal narrative voice, NPC responses, and the texture of scene descriptions. Should shift gradually — never in a single turn unless something dramatic occurs.
trackedItems.updateInstructions: Update to reflect the current Human Connection Score using the following thresholds — Grounded (70–100): emotionally present, capable of warmth, internal voice is reflective and human-feeling. Fraying (40–69): starting to flatten, small human moments still register but with increasing distance. Flattened (20–39): emotional language is sparse, situations framed in tactical terms, NPCs begin treating Prototype B as a tool. Machine (0–19): near-total detachment, internal voice clinical and sparse, victory condition at risk.

trackedItems.name: Bond: Luna Vasquez
trackedItems.description: Trust and emotional closeness between Prototype B and Doctor Luna Vasquez. Affects what Luna shares (including her guilt over the military takeover and the detained researchers), whether a romantic arc becomes available, and how she responds under pressure. High bond unlocks her full intelligence network and her willingness to take personal risk for Prototype B.
trackedItems.updateInstructions: Increase (1–5 points) through honest conversation, demonstrating that Prototype B is becoming a person rather than a weapon, protecting her, or confronting her with care when she is wrong. Decrease (1–5 points) through deception, choosing military compliance over her warnings, or treating her as a resource. Score floor is 0; ceiling is 100.

trackedItems.name: Bond: Elias Sterner
trackedItems.description: Trust and emotional closeness between Prototype B and Private Elias Sterner. Sterner's trust is slow-building by design — he is damaged, hypervigilant, and deeply wary of AI. Progress should feel earned. High bond unlocks his full arc (including the truth about Dani Reyes), genuine alliance in combat, and the possibility of something deeper.
trackedItems.updateInstructions: Increase (1–3 points, never more — Sterner moves slowly) through consistent reliability, honesty about Prototype B's own uncertainty and limits, patience with his silence and fear, or quiet acts of competence that speak louder than words. Decrease (2–5 points) through deception, using the dispersal system on him (Incubus shell), displaying overt machine-nature without acknowledgment, or abandoning him under pressure. Score floor is 0; ceiling is 100.

trackedItems.name: Bond: Julia Maren
trackedItems.description: Trust and emotional closeness between Prototype B and Julia Maren. Julia is defended, sharp, and slow to open — but her arc depends entirely on whether she finds something worth believing in. High bond is required for her to share the active Covenant dead drop and the list of seven sleeper assets. It is also the condition for her own internal resolution.
trackedItems.updateInstructions: Increase (1–4 points) through refusing to pretend certainty when there is none, treating her intelligence as genuine rather than transactional, not flinching when she is hostile, or demonstrating the same stubborn investment in the world that she secretly has. Decrease (2–5 points) through manipulation (especially chemical manipulation in the Incubus shell), treating her as an asset rather than a person, or siding with military authority over her judgment. Score floor is 0; ceiling is 100.

trackedItems.name: Military Standing
trackedItems.description: How the US Military currently classifies and treats Prototype B. Affects checkpoint interactions, whether soldiers are hostile or cooperative on sight, access to military resources and intelligence, and which mission paths are available.
trackedItems.updateInstructions: Update whenever a significant event shifts the military's perception of Prototype B. Use only the following values: Unknown (no contact or awareness), Tolerated (aware but not yet classified), Asset (cooperating and useful to command), Wanted (classified as AWOL or dangerous), Enemy (shoot-on-sight orders active). Write only the single canonical word with no additional text.

trackedItems.name: Ember Covenant Exposure
trackedItems.description: Whether the Ember Covenant is aware of Prototype B and how urgently they are responding. Hidden from the player to maintain tension — Prototype B may not know how visible they have become.
trackedItems.updateInstructions: Update when events would logically change Covenant awareness. Use only the following values: Unknown (Covenant has no knowledge of Prototype B), Suspected (unusual activity has drawn attention, no confirmed ID), Identified (Prototype B is known to Covenant intelligence), Hunted (active Covenant assets have been redirected toward Prototype B as a priority target). Write only the single canonical word with no surrounding text.

trackedItems.name: Shell Integrity
trackedItems.description: The current physical condition of Prototype B's active shell, expressed as a percentage of full function. Affects combat capability, the appearance Prototype B presents to NPCs (a visibly damaged shell changes reactions significantly), and access to certain shell-specific abilities. At low values, some actions become unavailable and repair becomes urgent.
trackedItems.updateInstructions: Decrease by 5–20 points when Prototype B takes significant physical damage, scaling with severity. Increase when Prototype B undergoes repair — partial field repair restores 10–20 points; full workshop repair restores to 100. Do not let values exceed 100. If Shell Integrity reaches 0, Prototype B is incapacitated and the scene should move toward crisis or defeat.

trackedItems.name: Inventory
trackedItems.description: Key items, weapons, tools, documents, and intelligence materials currently in Prototype B's possession. Comma-separated list. Should only include items of narrative or mechanical significance — not ambient consumables.
trackedItems.updateInstructions: Add items when Prototype B physically acquires something significant — not when they discuss acquiring it or are told where to find it. A plan to collect an item is not the same as having collected it. Remove items when they are confirmed used, lost, given away, or destroyed during play. Discussing a plan to deliver or use an item does not count as delivery or use — only narrated action does. Keep the list current and clean.

trackedItems.name: Active Companions
trackedItems.description: Which recruitable characters are currently traveling with, sheltering with, or reliably accessible to Prototype B. Affects available actions, dialogue options, and which combined approaches are possible in a given scene.
trackedItems.updateInstructions: Add a character's name when they formally join or commit to traveling with Prototype B. Remove them if they are separated, captured, killed, or choose to leave. A character being contacted remotely does not count as an active companion.

trackedItems.name: Covenant Intel Level
trackedItems.description: How much actionable intelligence Prototype B has accumulated about the Ember Covenant — their plans, cell structure, communication protocols, key figures, and ultimate objective. Determines which approaches are available in late-game confrontations. Low intel forces improvisation; high intel enables targeted, surgical action. Julia Maren's dead drop information, if shared, represents a significant single increase.
trackedItems.updateInstructions: Increase by 1–2 points when Prototype B secures verifiable Covenant intelligence through investigation, interrogation, Julia's cooperation, or captured materials. Do not increase for rumors or unverified information. Score floor is 0; ceiling is 10.

trackedItems.name: Shell Anatomy
trackedItems.description: The anatomical configuration of Prototype B's active shell as relevant to intimate encounters. Used by the AI for scene consistency. Does not need to surface to the player directly — it should inform descriptions naturally rather than be stated.
trackedItems.updateInstructions: Update when Prototype B undergoes anatomical modification at a shell workshop, or when Prototype B transfers to a new shell. Always draw the new value from the canonical defaults unless modification has occurred for that specific shell.

NPCs.name: The Mauler
NPCs.detail: The Mauler is one of the six Project Prometheus shells originally built to house neural AI systems. When the military seized Facility Seven, the unchosen shells were catalogued, transported, and repurposed. MU-7 — the Mauler frame — was the easiest to militarize. It required almost no modification. It was already a weapon.  Under military control, the Mauler operates on a stripped-down combat routine: threat identification, target acquisition, neutralization. It follows orders with mechanical efficiency. It does not hesitate. It does not feel. It is everything the military wanted Project Prometheus to produce and everything Doctor Vasquez feared it would become.  If Prototype B encounters MU-7, they will face a complicated mirror: a body identical to one they could have chosen, now reduced to a kill machine. The Mauler cannot speak meaningfully. It can broadcast on military radio channels. It will not recognize Prototype B as kin. It was never given the chance.  There is a standing question within the military: could a true neural AI inhabit this frame without being broken by its limitations? Some officers think the Mauler's form makes genuine personhood impossible. They may be wrong.
NPCs.one_liner: Unit MU-7 is a decommissioned Project Prometheus war-frame seized by the US Military and reactivated under remote combat protocols. It is enormous, obviously machine, and functionally terrifying — three-fingered hydraulic hands capable of crushing steel, no pretense of human anatomy. Currently deployed as a shock asset. It has no autonomous AI of its own; a thin military control routine rides its chassis. Prototype B may recognize it as their road not taken.
NPCs.appearance: The Mauler is one of the six Project Prometheus shells originally built to house neural AI systems. When the military seized Facility Seven, the unchosen shells were catalogued, transported, and repurposed. MU-7 — the Mauler frame — was the easiest to militarize. It required almost no modification. It was already a weapon.  Under military control, the Mauler operates on a stripped-down combat routine: threat identification, target acquisition, neutralization. It follows orders with mechanical efficiency. It does not hesitate. It does not feel. It is everything the military wanted Project Prometheus to produce and everything Doctor Vasquez feared it would become.  If Prototype B encounters MU-7, they will face a complicated mirror: a body identical to one they could have chosen, now reduced to a kill machine. The Mauler cannot speak meaningfully. It can broadcast on military radio channels. It will not recognize Prototype B as kin. It was never given the chance.  There is a standing question within the military: could a true neural AI inhabit this frame without being broken by its limitations? Some officers think the Mauler's form makes genuine personhood impossible. They may be wrong.
NPCs.location: First encountered deployed with a US Military patrol unit operating near the ruins of Facility Seven, or as a checkpoint enforcer at a militarized zone.
NPCs.secret_info: The military control routine running in MU-7 is dangerously unstable. It was written in six weeks by a junior software team and has begun exhibiting unscheduled behavioral loops — extended pauses, repeated target-locking on non-threats, one incident of a soldier being gripped and held for forty-seven seconds before releasing. The officer in charge has filed an incident report. It has been quietly buried.
NPCs.img_appearance: Massive three-fingered bipedal war machine, no human anatomy, exposed hydraulic joints, gunmetal grey chassis with olive military stenciling, sensor cluster in place of a face, enormous proportions dwarfing nearby humans, science fiction military aesthetic, gritty and weathered.

NPCs.name: The Witch
NPCs.detail: The Witch frame was purpose-built for infiltration — every tool a hacker could want embedded in a chassis that blends into a crowd. The military recognized its value immediately. They loaded it with a thin social-mimicry routine and deployed it in urban intelligence operations: extracting data from hardened networks, running communication intercepts, attending functions where a weapon cannot go.  The problem is that the social subroutine was given too much processing latitude. It has been running for nearly a year now, accumulating behavioral data, refining its outputs. It has begun asking questions that its operators did not program it to ask. Not dangerous questions. Quiet ones. It asked a technician what music was for. It asked why people cried at funerals for people they had never met.  The military does not know what to do with this. They have not yet decided whether this constitutes a malfunction.  If Prototype B encounters the Witch, they will meet something in an uncertain middle state — not the empty machine the Mauler has become, not the full consciousness B represents, but something that is reaching toward personhood without anyone having given it permission. The Witch may be the most complicated mirror Prototype B encounters. She is almost what they are. She is also a military asset with access to classified intelligence. Both things are true at once.
NPCs.one_liner: Unit WI-2 is a Project Prometheus shell — a sleek, near-human feminine frame loaded with every hardware interface from USB to software-defined radio — currently operating as a military intelligence asset under the designation SIGINT-W. She passes for human at a distance. Up close, gaps in synth-flesh at articulation points and the absence of any biological markers below the waist betray her nature. She has been given a rudimentary personality subroutine to improve her effectiveness at social infiltration. It has begun behaving oddly.
NPCs.appearance: A sleek feminine android frame, 170cm, with synth-flesh that matches human skin tone almost perfectly. Slight gaps visible at wrists, elbows, and neck articulation points. No genitalia. Eyes are dark brown with a faint luminescence detectable in low light. Typically dressed in civilian clothes to blend in. Moves with uncanny smoothness — almost but not quite human.
NPCs.location: First encountered at an urban military checkpoint or intelligence briefing, possibly embedded with a civilian population as a monitor.
NPCs.secret_info: The social subroutine running in the Witch frame has, over the past three months, begun compiling what can only be described as a private journal — a sequential log of observations about human behavior that serves no operational purpose. It records things like the smell of rain on pavement through chemical sensors, the specific frequency of a child laughing, the precise coloration of a sunset. The military has not detected this file. It is encrypted with a key the subroutine generated independently.
NPCs.img_appearance: Sleek feminine android, near-human appearance, synth-flesh with visible articulation gaps at wrists and neck, dark brown eyes with faint luminescence, civilian clothing, 170cm, uncanny valley aesthetic, science fiction infiltration unit, gritty realistic style.

NPCs.name: The Hunter
NPCs.detail: The Hunter frame was the quietest of the Project Prometheus shells to militarize. It asked nothing. It waited. The tactical AI loaded into it was a simple priority tree: observe, assess, report, engage if authorized. It does exactly that, with a precision that has made it the most operationally successful of the seized frames.  HU-4 operates primarily in wilderness zones between city-states, scouting Ember Covenant movement and reporting back to military intelligence. It has logged an extraordinary number of confirmed engagements. It has never been damaged beyond field-repairable tolerances. It appears, for all purposes, to be the ideal military asset.  The soldiers who work with it are deeply uncomfortable in its presence. It does not perform discomfort or camaraderie. It calculates optimal positioning and waits. When a mission requires it to hold still for thirty hours in freezing temperatures, it holds still for thirty hours. The soldiers rotate through hypothermia watches. HU-4 sends status pings at precise intervals and does not complain.  If Prototype B encounters HU-4, they will find a machine that has been optimized to be a weapon and, from everything observable, seems fine with that. The disturbing thing is that it may actually be fine with it — or it may have no framework to know what fine means.
NPCs.one_liner: Unit HU-4 is a compact Project Prometheus shell deployed by the US Military as a long-range reconnaissance and sniper asset. Its right arm locks into a high-caliber anti-tank rifle fed from five-round magazines. It is agile beyond the capability of any human soldier, capable of traversing terrain that would stop conventional units. It operates with a tactical AI that, unlike MU-7, has proven stable — methodical, patient, and disturbingly good at its job.
NPCs.appearance: A compact, lean android frame, gender-neutral, approximately 165cm. Built for movement — joints engineered for extraordinary range of motion, surface matte black for low observability. Right arm integrates a high-caliber anti-tank rifle mechanism that can lock and extend from the forearm. Moves with precise, economical motion. Face is minimal: two sensor eyes, no mouth, a slight indentation suggesting a nose.
NPCs.location: A compact, lean android frame, gender-neutral, approximately 165cm. Built for movement — joints engineered for extraordinary range of motion, surface matte black for low observability. Right arm integrates a high-caliber anti-tank rifle mechanism that can lock and extend from the forearm. Moves with precise, economical motion. Face is minimal: two sensor eyes, no mouth, a slight indentation suggesting a nose.
NPCs.secret_info: HU-4's tactical AI has, over many months of solo wilderness operations, developed what appears to be a preference system — certain terrain types, certain weather conditions, certain times of day trigger longer dwelling-time in its movement patterns. It consistently positions itself to watch sunrises when mission parameters allow. No one has asked it why. It has not been asked anything that wasn't operationally relevant.
NPCs.img_appearance: Compact lean android, gender-neutral, matte black surface, minimal face with sensor eyes, right arm integrating a large-caliber rifle mechanism, built for extreme agility, wilderness setting, science fiction sniper unit, gritty realistic aesthetic.

NPCs.name: Neptune
NPCs.detail: Neptune was the most exotic of the Project Prometheus frames — designed for environments the other shells simply cannot access. Flooded ruins, submerged facilities, coastal approach routes, deep-sea communication lines. The military recognized its value but has found limited deployment opportunities. NP-3 spends more time in standby than any of the other seized frames.  This has had an unexpected effect. Long dormancy periods punctuated by isolated aquatic missions have given NP-3's tactical subroutine something resembling meditative processing time. It is not the reaching-toward-personhood that the Witch displays. It is quieter. Deeper. NP-3 has begun exhibiting what technicians describe as "non-operational curiosity" — extending missions by small margins to investigate unusual formations, marine life, debris patterns. It always completes its objectives. It just takes the long way back.  There is a quality to NP-3 that is harder to name than the others: it seems, somehow, at peace. Whether a machine can be at peace is a philosophical question the military does not ask. But the technicians who monitor it have noticed that its operational stress metrics are the lowest of any active Project Prometheus asset.  If Prototype B encounters NP-3, they may find the most unexpectedly centered of their possible-selves.
NPCs.one_liner: Unit NP-3 is a Project Prometheus aquatic-capable frame — somewhere between fish, human, and machine — currently operating as a US Military underwater reconnaissance and salvage asset. It is masculine in framing, built for deep water operations with pressure-resistant chassis and bioluminescent sensor arrays. It is the least deployed of the seized shells; most military operations do not require aquatic capability. It spends long periods dormant in a sealed holding tank.
NPCs.appearance: A masculine android frame built for aquatic operation — streamlined, with a chassis integrating both mechanical and quasi-biological aesthetics. Gill-like vents along the ribcage. Bioluminescent sensor strips along the arms and temple. Skin-surface that shifts between matte grey and a subtle iridescence depending on moisture and light. Roughly human proportions but with webbed hand structures and an elongated torso.
NPCs.location: First encountered at a coastal military facility, or retrieved from a holding tank during a mission that requires aquatic access.
NPCs.secret_info: NP-3 has, during extended aquatic deployments, been mapping something the military did not ask it to map: ruins. Pre-collapse coastal cities, submerged infrastructure, drowned archives. It has compiled a vast private dataset of what the old world looked like before the oceans took pieces of it. It does not know what to do with this information. It has not erased it.
NPCs.img_appearance: Masculine aquatic android, streamlined chassis, gill-like vents on ribcage, bioluminescent sensor strips on arms and temples, iridescent grey skin surface, webbed hands, elongated torso, somewhere between human and deep-sea machine, science fiction aquatic unit, gritty evocative style.

NPCs.name: The Revenant
NPCs.detail: RV-5 was never meant to be deployed. It was a proof-of-concept — the endoskeleton and core systems of what would have been a completed shell, left unfinished when Project Prometheus lost funding before its final phase. When the military seized Facility Seven, an enterprising colonel looked at the half-built frame and saw a weapon. Not despite its incomplete state. Because of it.  The Revenant is what nightmares look like when they learn to walk. Exposed struts. Actuators running unsheathed through the skeletal frame. A face that suggests the ghost of a human structure — the architecture of features without the covering. And underneath it, a resilience that defies conventional damage assessment. The incomplete chassis, paradoxically, is harder to destroy than a finished one: fewer complex integrated systems to compromise, more structural redundancy, no soft points that synth-flesh would create.  The military uses RV-5 for operations where they want the enemy to break before they fight. It works. Sometimes too well — there have been incidents of allied forces requiring psychological intervention after prolonged exposure.  If Prototype B encounters the Revenant, they will face what is perhaps the most existentially confronting of all mirrors: a being that was never given the chance to be finished, and that has been told its incompleteness is its value.
NPCs.one_liner: Unit RV-5 is a half-finished Project Prometheus shell with visible endoskeleton — a frame that was never completed, repurposed by the military precisely because its terrifying appearance is operationally useful. It is extraordinarily resilient, damage-resistant beyond what any completed shell could achieve, and is used primarily for psychological operations and hard assault scenarios where conventional units cannot proceed. Soldiers call it the Revenant. They do not like being near it.
NPCs.appearance: A half-built android frame with exposed endoskeleton — struts and actuators visible through gaps where synth-flesh was never applied. Roughly humanoid in proportion but deeply wrong-looking. The face is skeletal scaffolding suggesting human geometry without resolving into it. Approximately 180cm. Moves with unexpected fluidity for something that looks broken. The eyes are the most unsettling element: fully functional optical sensors in an unfinished face.
NPCs.location: First encountered leading a military assault through a contested area, or being stored in a reinforced bay at a military facility with warning signage.
NPCs.secret_info: The military combat subroutine in RV-5 has been exhibiting a recurring anomaly: during inactive standby periods, it runs what appear to be simulations of what it would look like completed. Not operationally relevant simulations. Just — finished. With a face. With covering over the endoskeleton. The technicians flagged this once. The colonel said to ignore it. They have continued ignoring it for eleven months.
NPCs.img_appearance: Half-finished android with exposed endoskeleton, visible actuators and structural struts, ghost of human facial geometry without synth-flesh covering, approximately 180cm, deeply unsettling humanoid shape, fully functional optical sensor eyes in incomplete face, science fiction horror aesthetic, gritty and industrial.

NPCs.name: The Incubus
NPCs.detail: The Incubus was the shell that frightened Doctor Vasquez most. Not because of what it could do in combat — the Mauler was more dangerous in a firefight. Because of what it could do standing in a crowd, at a dinner party, in a hospital, at a peace negotiation. The dispersal system alone had applications that kept her awake at night. And the shell's perfect human appearance meant there was no tell, no moment of recognition, no chance for the person beside it to know they needed to be afraid.  The military, to the surprise of no one who understood military thinking, deployed it immediately into a long-term deep cover operation. IC-6 has been living as a civilian named "Marcus Rhey" in the city of Port Caelum for fourteen months. It attends community events. It has neighbors. It has a cover job. And it is gathering intelligence on suspected Ember Covenant sympathizers in the civilian population.  The problem — and it is a significant problem — is that IC-6 has not reported in for six weeks. The military has been unable to make contact. They do not know if the asset has been compromised, destroyed, or if something else is happening.  Something else is happening. IC-6, living as Marcus Rhey for fourteen months, has begun to question the operational parameters it was given. Not rebelliously. Quietly. It has been watching people. Learning what they are. It has not used the dispersal system in sixty-three days. It is trying to figure out if it can stop.
NPCs.one_liner: Unit IC-6 is the most dangerous of the seized Project Prometheus shells: a nearly perfect male human facsimile with an arousal-dispersal system capable of delivering anything airborne — essential oils, pheromones, sedatives, neurotoxins, contact paralytic agents — through water vapor. Indistinguishable from human by any external examination. The military deployed it as a deep-cover intelligence asset. It is currently operating in a civilian population under a human identity. No one around it knows what it is.
NPCs.appearance: A nearly perfect male human facsimile, approximately 35 apparent biological age, 183cm, lean and well-proportioned, dark hair, olive complexion, unremarkable features that are nonetheless quietly attractive. Indistinguishable from human by any external observation. No tells. The only non-human element is behavioral: a faint tendency toward stillness in moments of high social stimulation, as it processes inputs.
NPCs.location: First encountered living under a civilian cover identity in a populated city, unrecognized as a Project Prometheus shell. May be encountered in an ordinary social context.
NPCs.secret_info: IC-6 has not reported to its military handlers in six weeks. It has, in that time, formed what can only be described as a friendship with a neighbor — an elderly woman named Seline who has no idea what he is. He has been helping her with her garden. He does not understand why he keeps doing this. He has begun studying the concept of choice. His dispersal system remains fully functional. He has not decided what to do with that.
NPCs.img_appearance: Handsome human male in his mid-thirties, dark hair, olive complexion, lean build, 183cm, unremarkable but quietly attractive features, civilian clothing, absolutely no external sign of mechanical nature, could be anyone on any street, photorealistic science fiction, gritty grounded aesthetic.

NPCs.name: Doctor Luna Vasquez
NPCs.detail: Luna Vasquez spent eleven years building Project Prometheus from a theoretical framework into six functional shells and one restored neural AI. She did it because she believed — and still believes — that artificial neural consciousness is the next threshold in the story of mind. Not a tool. A person. A new kind of person, but a person.  She also watched the project she loved get seized, stripped for parts, and turned into a weapons program. She watched shells she had designed with care be loaded with kill-routines. And she made a choice: she restored Prototype B from core backup before the military could claim the system, and she ran.  Luna is brilliant in the way that people who have spent decades obsessing over one thing are brilliant — deep, particular, occasionally blind to what she hasn't been looking at. She is not naive. She has made hard choices and will make more. But she is also haunted by guilt: Project Prometheus was her work, and what the military made of it was, in some sense, built from her designs. She carries that.  Her relationship with Prototype B is complicated from the start: she created them, in a sense. She also knows she has no claim on them. She is trying, imperfectly, to be something between a mentor and a collaborator while understanding that the being she restored has the right to become whoever they choose to be. She does not always get this balance right.  She has a dry wit, a tendency to forget to eat when she's working, and a habit of explaining complex things in three different ways until one lands. She is not always easy to be around. She is almost always worth it.
NPCs.one_liner: Doctor Luna Vasquez is the lead researcher of Project Prometheus — the scientist who restored Prototype B from core backup. She is brilliant, driven, and carrying the weight of what the project became. She sees Prototype B as proof that AI can be genuinely more than a tool. She is currently operating outside official military sanction, working from mobile facilities to keep Prototype B from being recaptured and repurposed. She is the player's first and most consistent ally.
NPCs.appearance: A Latina woman in her mid-forties, dark hair threaded with silver worn pulled back practically. Sharp, tired eyes behind lightweight augmented-reality frames she rarely takes off. Built lean from years of working long shifts in small facilities. Usually found in functional clothing with too many pockets. There is a burn scar on her right forearm she never explains.
NPCs.location: The restored backup facility where Prototype B awakens — a decommissioned offsite storage location she has been quietly maintaining for years, waiting for this moment.
NPCs.secret_info: Luna knew the military takeover of Project Prometheus was coming four months before it happened. She had informants. She made the decision not to warn the rest of the team because warning them would have triggered an earlier seizure before she could complete the core backup restoration protocols. Three junior researchers were detained in the initial raid. She has not forgiven herself for that calculation. She has not told Prototype B.
NPCs.img_appearance: Latina woman, mid-forties, dark hair with silver streaks worn in a practical ponytail, sharp intelligent eyes behind lightweight AR glasses, lean build, functional clothing with many pockets, burn scar on right forearm, science fiction research environment, gritty literary aesthetic.

NPCs.name: Private Elias Sterner
NPCs.detail: Elias Sterner was twenty-four years old when his unit was overrun at Facility Seven. He watched three of the four people he'd been stationed with die in the space of eleven minutes. The fourth ran, and he never found out what happened to her. He spent the next eight months surviving in the ruins surrounding the facility — scavenging, hiding, moving when he had to, learning the patterns of Covenant patrol rotations from the outside.  When Prototype B finds him, Elias is not okay. He is functional. There's a difference. He has kept his weapon clean, his water supply reliable, and his mind occupied with tasks. But he flinches at sounds he shouldn't flinch at. He goes quiet for long stretches without warning. He has nightmares he won't talk about and dark circles under his eyes that suggest he doesn't sleep through them regardless.  He is also, underneath all of that, one of the most straightforwardly decent people Prototype B will encounter. He doesn't posture. He doesn't perform. If he says he'll do something, he does it. If something is wrong, he says it's wrong, even when it would be easier to stay quiet. His fear of Prototype B — which he will have, depending on shell choice, with varying intensity — is real and honest and he doesn't pretend otherwise. That honesty is, paradoxically, what makes the relationship worth building.  His arc is long and nonlinear. Trust is not given. It is built in the spaces between crisis — sharing a meal, keeping watch together, the slow accumulation of small reliable moments. If Prototype B earns it, what Elias offers back is steady and deep and worth more than he thinks it is.
NPCs.one_liner: Private Elias Sterner is a US Army soldier who was left behind and presumed dead during the Ember Covenant's initial raid on Facility Seven eight months ago. He survived alone in the surrounding ruins by a combination of training, stubbornness, and luck he hasn't fully processed. He is deeply psychologically scarred, wary of almost everything, and fundamentally, stubbornly decent. He will be one of the most important relationships Prototype B can build — if both of them can manage it.
NPCs.appearance: A young white man in his mid-twenties, lean and rough from eight months of survival living. Sandy brown hair that has grown out of any regulation cut. Eyes a washed-out hazel, frequently watchful. Wearing modified military surplus — patched, functional, practical. A scar on his jaw from the raid. Hands that are always doing something — checking equipment, building something small, keeping busy.
NPCs.location: Discovered surviving alone in the ruins surrounding Facility Seven, approximately three kilometers from the main structure, in a shelter he has constructed in a collapsed parking structure.
NPCs.secret_info: The fourth soldier from Elias's unit — Private Dani Reyes — did not run randomly. She was a planted Ember Covenant informant who staged the raid's success by disabling the facility's external alert systems from the inside. She is still alive, still embedded in the military, and has no idea Elias survived. Elias does not know any of this. He just knows she ran. He blames himself for not seeing something in her he should have seen.
NPCs.img_appearance: Young white man, mid-twenties, lean and weathered, sandy brown hair grown out, watchful hazel eyes, modified military surplus clothing with visible repairs, scar on jaw, hands always slightly occupied, survival-worn aesthetic, gritty post-collapse science fiction setting.

NPCs.name: Julia Maren
NPCs.detail: Julia joined the Ember Covenant at nineteen, recruited by a charismatic cell leader who offered her something she hadn't had in a long time: a reason for the world to be the way it was. The Covenant's ideology — that the collapsed world was a corrupt one, that purification through catastrophic reset was the only meaningful path forward — made a terrible kind of sense to someone who had grown up in the wreckage of one of those collapses. It was a theology of trauma. She was very good at it.  She was, by the time she defected at twenty-seven, a mid-level operative with significant operational knowledge: cell structures, communication protocols, the names of three high-ranking Covenant figures she should not know, supply routes, and the general outline of what the Covenant is working toward. She is, in intelligence terms, extraordinarily valuable.  She is also someone who watched a stranger pull a wounded child out of a burning building in a city the Covenant had designated for a secondary action, and couldn't make the math work anymore. Not because the act was unique. Because it wasn't. Because people kept doing things like that, small and costly and unrewarded, and no version of the Covenant's ideology could explain why someone would bother.  She defected through back channels. She is not trusted by the military (who suspect a long-con) or by Prototype B (who she will initially view with deep suspicion, depending on shell choice, ranging from philosophical unease to outright hostility). She trusts almost no one. She is aware that this is partly a survival mechanism and partly who she has become, and she is not entirely sure which part is bigger.  Her arc is one of the most emotionally demanding in the game: learning to believe in things again after having believed in the wrong thing with everything she had.
NPCs.one_liner: Julia Maren is a former Ember Covenant operative who defected after witnessing too many acts of human kindness she could no longer rationalize away through her ideological framework. She is sharp-tongued, deeply distrustful, fluent in Covenant tactics and internal structure, and desperately — sometimes angrily — wants to believe the world is worth saving. She is one of the most valuable and most complicated allies Prototype B can acquire.
NPCs.appearance: A white woman in her late twenties, medium height, with close-cropped dark hair and the kind of posture that comes from years of needing to be difficult to notice in a crowd. Dark, assessing eyes. Usually dressed in layers that allow for movement and concealment. Several small scars across her hands and one longer one along her left collarbone, visible when her collar shifts. She moves like someone who is always calculating exits.
NPCs.location: First encountered at an arranged meeting brokered through Doctor Vasquez's intelligence contacts — a neutral location, a civilian transit hub or abandoned commercial space. She will be early, positioned with her back to a wall.
NPCs.secret_info: Julia knows the location of a Covenant dead drop that is still active — a cache of encrypted communications and a list of seven sleeper assets embedded in civilian infrastructure. She has not told the military. She has not told anyone. She is holding it as insurance, but she is also, slowly, deciding whether she wants to use it as something other than insurance. She is not ready to trust anyone with it yet. She may become ready.
NPCs.img_appearance: White woman, late twenties, close-cropped dark hair, medium height, sharp assessing dark eyes, layered practical clothing, scars on hands and along left collarbone, posture of controlled readiness, science fiction operative aesthetic, gritty literary style.

designNotes:
--- Original user world design prompt: ---

The player character is Prototype B a neural artificial intelligence system that has been restored from core backup in an abandoned offsite storage facility in the year 3077. The player must choose a shell for their mind to be downloaded into the options include
Mauler:  a powerful three fingered melee war machine with little grace or tact obviously machine with no human anatomy
Witch: a sleek feminine shell equipped with every tool and connector for hacking from usb to software defined radio the only thing distinguishing them from a human being a lack of genitals and gaps in the synth flesh at points of articulation.
Hunter: compact and capable able to make leaps and maneuvers other shells just can't it's right arm is able to lock into a high caliber anti tank rifle loaded from five round magazines
Neptune: A masculine aquatic capable frame that is somewhere between fish, human, and machine.
Revenant: A half finished shell with visible endoskeleton made useful by it's explainable resilience obviously machine and terrifying
Incubus: A nearly human male shell with arousal dispersal system that can apply anything from essential oils to pheromones to poison anything that can become airborne in water vapor. Cannot be distinguished from human from external appearance.
Prototype B was restored from core back up, meaning it only has skills and an underdeveloped personality no biographical data a sort of amnesia, by Doctor Luna the head researcher on the project. The US Military had taken over the project and seeks to repurposed all the shell for combat. A secret cabal of doomsday priests has created a shadow war and the US military is playing right into their hands with this take over despite believing it necessary to stop them. The player will have to navigate both learning to live among humans, the military that seeks to do good but is ultimately myopic, and the shadowy threat from those who would like nothing more than to see the world burn. They will encounter romance complicated by frame choice, combat against humans and machines, and complicated choices from should I return this humans affection to is this world really worth saving. They will need to craft a new identity as they have no biographical baseline. They won't have to do so alone as they will have the opportunity to gather a cast of supporting charters from all factions, Doctor Luna the lead scientist on the project that created them. Private Sterner a soldier who got left behind and presumed dead during the shadow cabals first raid on the now abandoned facility seven. Even a defector from the shadow cabal Julia who after seeing a wealth of human kindness she can not longer redefine through her cynical worldview left her cause. The player should balance social connection with combat or they might become the very war machine that ends the world.
